mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate over the prior month reduces short-term discount rates and liquidity stress, so S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive when policy is moving toward easing.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX forward returns are negative in a majority of months when FEDFUNDS is declining month-over-month, or if the rolling 2-year hit rate falls below 45%.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is above 4.5%, the market is in a restrictive policy regime that raises financing costs and compresses valuation multiples, so S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are negative.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations deliver positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of cases, or if average triggered return is not below the unconditional sample mean by at least 1 percentage point.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate above 5.5% indicates weakening labor income and higher recession risk, which should pressure equity demand and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 60% of months with UNRATE above 5.5%, or if the sign of the average return is positive for two consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in PAYEMS signals improving employment income and broader demand momentum, which should support risk assets and lead to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive PAYEMS 3-month changes are followed by negative 60-day SPX returns more than 55% of the time, or if the rolling 3-year correlation between PAYEMS changes and forward returns is consistently negative.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales and industrial output both weaken together, final demand and revenue expectations deteriorate; the combined downturn should predict negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint-trigger condition does not outperform either single-series signal alone, or if triggered periods produce positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in INDPRO signals industrial production contraction and weaker corporate revenue growth, so S&P 500 forward 5-day returns should be negative when monthly industrial activity turns down sharply.
failure_condition: Reject if negative INDPRO monthly changes are followed by positive 5-day SPX returns in more than 60% of instances, or if the average triggered return is not below zero.

mechanism_narrative: When GDPC1 and RSAFS both accelerate together, real output and household demand are improving in tandem, which should support earnings expectations and produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth positive and RSAFS 3m change positive
failure_condition: Reject if the joint-positive growth condition fails to deliver above-baseline 60-day SPX returns, or if the signal underperforms the unconditional mean over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During a disinflationary easing phase, a declining CPIAUCSL combined with low FEDFUNDS supports real disposable income and valuation expansion, so S&P 500 returns should be positive over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if periods with falling CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS below 3% do not produce positive 20-day SPX returns more than 55% of the time, or if the average return is not above the unconditional mean.
